stories.title: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff  that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
stories.simple_summary: Author Dave Eggers visited OpenAI and told the company's staff that AI like ChatGPT is preventing young writers from developing their skills and voices. He's concerned that AI is replacing the opportunity for new generations to learn and practice writing.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're in a creative field or teaching young people, this raises a real question: will AI tools replace people doing creative work, or will it just change how they work?

stories.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
stories.simple_summary: A Chinese company made a new AI tool called Kimi. Some people are worried about what it means for competition and control in AI technology.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and tech companies worried about competition — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: The apps, gadgets, and tools every reader needs
stories.simple_summary: This is a list of recommendations for apps and gadgets that readers might find useful. It's a shopping guide rather than news.
stories.why_it_matters: Nothing urgent here — just optional shopping recommendations for people interested in tools.

stories.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
stories.simple_summary: A wealthy investor predicts that the huge profits being made from AI will eventually have to be shared more widely with society, either by choice or by force.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and policy-makers — nothing you need to act on right now.

stories.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
stories.simple_summary: A luxury phone company is selling an expensive phone with an AI helper for $6,880, and reviewers tested how well it actually works.
stories.why_it_matters: This is only relevant if you're thinking about buying a very expensive luxury phone — most people don't need this product.

stories.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
stories.simple_summary: A company called Databricks (which helps other companies work with data and AI) is now worth $188 billion. It has repositioned itself as an AI company.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — it shows which companies are seen as winners in AI.

stories.title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
stories.simple_summary: There's a new feature for Zoom meetings that can record and summarize conversations. The headline questions whether recording everything actually helps people, or just creates too much information.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Zoom for business, it's worth thinking about whether automatic recordings of meetings help or just create clutter.

stories.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
stories.simple_summary: A robotics company called Agility is opening a training center for its robots (called Digit) in California, near where Tesla operates.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly relevant to engineers and investors in robotics — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
stories.simple_summary: Phone companies in India are struggling because AI tools take up so much computer memory inside phones. This is causing shortages and changing what phones cost.
stories.why_it_matters: If you buy smartphones, you might eventually see prices stay higher because AI features require more expensive computer chips inside them.

stories.title: TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
stories.simple_summary: TikTok is testing a new tool that lets creators find and report fake videos where AI has copied someone's face or voice without permission.
stories.why_it_matters: If you create videos online or are worried about deepfakes, this tool will give creators some protection against AI copies of their appearance.

stories.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
stories.simple_summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, claiming the company stole trade secrets and hired over 400 of Apple's former workers. This lawsuit could cause problems for OpenAI's plan to go public and become a publicly-traded company.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly relevant to investors and tech industry watchers — the lawsuit shows growing conflict between big tech companies over AI.

stories.title: Apple’s plot to crush OpenAI
stories.simple_summary: Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI. Experts disagree on how serious the claims are, but the timing is awkward since OpenAI is planning to sell stock to the public.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly relevant to investors and tech watchers — it shows big companies are fighting over AI control and money.
